sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: LMBO and REKT: Direxion debuts leveraged ETFs tracking crypto equities
feed.summary: Direxion has launched two new leveraged exchange-traded funds (ETFs) for the crypto industry, named LMBO and REKT. LMBO offers a 200% return tied to a crypto industry index, while REKT provides a 100% inverse return. These ETFs aim to allow traders to express short-term conviction on companies building the future of the crypto-driven economy.

feed.title: |‘Grocery demand is unprecedented’: Sprouts market breaks ground in Clarksville
feed.summary: |Construction has officially begun on Clarksville's first Sprouts Farmers Market, located at 575 Fire Station Road, with a groundbreaking ceremony held on Thursday. The 23,299-square-foot organic grocery store is projected to open in fall 2026 and will create over 80 local jobs. Community leaders emphasize that this project addresses the growing demand for healthy and organic food options in Montgomery County, signaling the area's strong population, purchasing power, and potential for further retail development.

feed.title: Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc. Announces Management Changes
feed.summary: Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc. announced significant management changes, appointing Don Clark as Chief Merchandising Officer and Amanda Rassi as Chief Customer Officer. Scott Neal, the current Chief Merchandising Officer, will retire. These appointments aim to enhance Sprouts' differentiated offerings and deepen its understanding and service to its customer base.

feed.title: Q4 2025 Open House: Opendoor 2.0 Does What It Said It Would Do - Delivering Acquisition Growth, Faster Inventory Turns, and Stronger Cohorts
feed.summary: Opendoor Technologies Inc. released its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results, highlighting significant progress under its "Opendoor 2.0" transformation plan. CEO Kaz Nejatian noted a 46% increase in homes purchased quarter-over-quarter, a 23% reduction in average days in possession, and stronger contribution margins from its October 2025 acquisition cohort. The company is focused on achieving breakeven Adjusted Net Income by the end of 2026 and provided guideposts for Q1 2026, including an expected increase in contribution margin.

feed.title: Papa Johns pizzeria to open in ParkWay Plaza in Norwalk, is 7th location in state
feed.summary: Papa Johns is opening its seventh Connecticut location in Norwalk's ParkWay Plaza at 345 Main Ave. The new independent franchise is currently listed as "temporarily closed" on the company's website, with an exact opening date pending. NBA great Shaquille O’Neal is a spokesperson and investor for the national pizza chain, which boasts over 6,000 restaurants worldwide.

feed.title: Hotchkis & Wiley Cuts Alphabet Inc. Class A Stake
feed.summary: Hotchkis & Wiley Capital Management LLC, managed by George Davis Jr, recently reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. Class A (GOOGL) by selling 623,685 shares. Despite this, Alphabet Class A shares have seen significant gains over the past year, with analysts maintaining a Strong Buy rating due to strong Q4 2025 results and leadership in AI, reflected in increasing capex. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, assigns GOOGL an "Outperform" score, citing financial quality but noting premium valuation and near-term capital intensity risks.

feed.title: Dr Reddy's Labs, CG Power, Oil India: How to trade these 3 buzzing stocks
feed.summary: An analyst from Master Capital Services provides trading recommendations for Dr Reddy's Labs, CG Power, and Oil India. Dr Reddy's Labs has seen a breakout above resistance, while CG Power shows a shift from bearish to bullish sentiment. Oil India has rebounded strongly from its 21-day EMA.

TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates GOOGL as "Outperform" due to its financial strength, though moderated by technical momentum and valuation.

feed.title: Indonesia sees US$11 bil in share sales to meet free-float to avoid downgrade
feed.summary: Indonesia is preparing for US$11 billion in share sales to meet stricter free-float requirements, aiming to prevent a potential downgrade to frontier market status by MSCI Inc. The Indonesia Stock Exchange estimates that 267 companies need to increase public shareholdings to 15% from 7.5%. This initiative follows MSCI's warning about investability and limited free float, prompting regulators to implement reforms to boost transparency and liquidity.

feed.title: Two Creeks Capital Management Boosts Amazon Stake by 147,575 Shares

Despite recent stock volatility and a massive capital expenditure plan, Wall Street analysts maintain "Strong Buy" ratings, citing AWS as a key growth driver. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates AMZN as "Neutral" due to strong financial performance tempered by weakened free cash flow conversion and a demanding valuation.

feed.title: (NBH) Movement as an Input in Quant Signal Sets
feed.summary: This article analyzes Neuberger Berman Municipal Fund Inc. (NBH) using quantitative AI models, noting a neutral sentiment across all time horizons. It outlines three distinct trading strategies—Position, Momentum Breakout, and Risk Hedging—with specific entry, target, and stop-loss levels. The analysis emphasizes a wait-and-see approach due to resistance testing and highlights a significant risk-reward short setup.

feed.title: Copart (CPRT) Reports Mixed Q2 2026 Results Amid Declines in Insurance Units
feed.summary: Copart (CPRT) reported mixed Q2 2026 results, with a decline in global insurance units but an increase in U.S. average selling prices. Despite a 3.6% decrease in consolidated revenue, the company maintains strong financial health, impressive operating margins, and a low debt-to-equity ratio. Valuation metrics suggest the stock is nearing historical lows, potentially indicating an opportunity for investors, though insider selling has been observed.

feed.title: Quanex Building Products Announces First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call Schedule
feed.summary: Quanex Building Products Corporation announced it will release its first quarter 2026 results on Thursday, March 5, 2026, after market close. A conference call to discuss these results is scheduled for Friday, March 6, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. ET. Investors can access the live audio webcast and replay on Quanex’s website.

feed.title: CSX CEO Touts AI Efficiency, U.S.-Led Growth and Service Gains at Barclays Industrial Conference
feed.summary: CSX CEO Steve discussed the company's focus on AI efficiency, U.S.-led growth, and service improvements at the Barclays Industrial Select Conference. He highlighted practical AI applications for improving efficiency and service, views the U.S. as the primary long-term growth engine, and emphasized operational progress and improving metrics despite customer concerns about consistency. CSX expects low-single-digit volume growth, driven by productivity and cost structure, and has simplified management incentives to focus on operating income growth, operating margin, and safety.

feed.title: Mairs & Power Inc Cuts Alphabet Stake by 64,619 Shares

TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates GOOG as Outperform, citing strong financial performance and technical trends despite concerns over future capital expenditure.

feed.title: Newmont Corporation Reports Decline in Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation reported a decline in its fourth-quarter profit to $1.301 billion, or $1.19 per share, from $1.403 billion and $1.24 per share last year, despite revenue increasing by 20.6% year-over-year to $6.818 billion. Adjusted earnings, excluding items, were $2.753 billion, or $2.52 per share. This decline in earnings, despite revenue growth, may negatively impact investor confidence given fluctuating metal prices.

feed.title: Tesla cuts price of Cybertruck model Cyberbeast in US
feed.summary: Tesla has reduced the price of its most expensive Cybertruck model, the Cyberbeast, to $99,990 from $114,990 in the U.S. This price cut suggests the discontinuation of the "Luxe Package" which included Supervised Full Self-Driving and free Supercharger access. Separately, Tesla also introduced a new, cheaper all-wheel-drive variant of its Model Y SUV as part of its strategy to attract more cost-conscious buyers.

feed.title: Klarna Deadline Tomorrow: KLAR Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Klarna Group plc Securities Lawsuit First Filed by The Rosen Law Firm
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm is reminding investors who purchased Klarna Group plc (NYSE: KLAR) securities of an approaching lead plaintiff deadline on February 20, 2026, in a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Klarna's registration statement for its September 2025 IPO contained false or misleading statements regarding the risk of increased loss reserves due to the nature of their "buy now, pay later" loans. Investors seeking to join the class action or serve as lead plaintiff are encouraged to contact the law firm.

feed.title: €10.09 CEMT.DE iShares MSCI Europe Mid-Cap (XETRA) pre-market 20 Feb 2026: oversold bounce possible
feed.summary: The iShares MSCI Europe Mid-Cap (CEMT.DE) is trading at €10.09 in XETRA pre-market on February 20, 2026, presenting an oversold bounce opportunity due to low-volume selling and its position above key moving averages. Meyka AI rates CEMT.DE with a score of 63.64 (Grade B, HOLD suggestion), projecting a quarterly target of €10.58 and a yearly target of €11.39. Traders are advised to manage risk tightly due to thin liquidity, using limit entries and tight stops for this tactical mean-reversion trade.

feed.title: Transocean cuts $1.3B debt in year it posts $2.9B net loss
feed.summary: Transocean (NYSE: RIG) reported full-year 2025 results with a net loss of $2.915 billion despite a 13% increase in contract drilling revenues to $3.965 billion and a 19% rise in Adjusted EBITDA to $1.37 billion. The significant net loss was primarily due to $3.036 billion in impairment charges. The company strengthened its balance sheet by reducing its total principal debt by $1.258 billion, or 18%, to $5.686 billion, and ended the year with a total backlog of approximately $6.1 billion.

feed.title: Top Northrop Grumman Executives Quietly Unload a Major Chunk of Stock
feed.summary: Two high-ranking Northrop Grumman executives, Michael Hardesty and Benjamin R. Davies, recently sold significant portions of their company stock, totaling over $2 million. Despite these insider sales, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates Northrop Grumman (NOC) as an "Outperform" due to strong financial performance, improving free cash flow, and bullish technical trends, although valuation is noted as a constraint. The company recently reported a record backlog and a favorable outlook for 2026.

feed.title: Top Neumora Executives Quietly Cash Out in Coordinated Stock Sell-Off
feed.summary: Top executives at Neumora Therapeutics (NMRA) recently engaged in coordinated stock sales, with CEO Paul Berns, COO Aurora Daljit Singh, and President Joshua Pinto cashing out shares totaling over $77,000. These transactions follow positive analyst attention due to favorable Phase Ib data for NMRA-511, a drug for Alzheimer’s disease agitation, which sparked a more optimistic valuation outlook for the company. Despite the insider selling, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, rates NMRA as "Neutral" due to weak financial performance tempered by constructive technical positioning and a promising pipeline.

feed.title: ‘China’s Netflix’ looks for stardom in new theme park business
feed.summary: iQiyi (IQ.US), often called "China's Netflix," is diversifying its revenue streams by launching a series of small-scale, immersive theme parks based on its streaming content. This move comes after seven quarters of declining revenue and aims to leverage its intellectual property in the growing experiential consumption market, following similar ventures by companies like Pop Mart, Miniso, and Netflix. While these parks are much smaller and cheaper to operate than traditional theme parks, iQiyi hopes they will attract new customers and boost its membership services.

feed.title: Edison Is Slashing Top Executive Bonuses After Deadly LA Fire
feed.summary: Edison International is reducing top executive bonuses by 40% following the deadly Eaton Fire in January 2025 that killed 19 people and destroyed over 10,000 structures in the Los Angeles area. CEO Pedro Pizarro and other senior executives will see their bonuses cut, and the company is also improving its wildfire compensation program to offer stronger support for displaced renters and better cover legal fees. This decision comes as the company faces lawsuits and awaits a state report on how wildfire mitigation costs should be distributed in California.

feed.title: Arista Networks’ Duda sells shares worth $4.5 million
feed.summary: Kenneth Duda, President and CTO of Arista Networks, sold $4.5 million worth of common stock on February 17, 2026, at prices between $141.1477 and $144.74. These sales were made directly, through a foundation, and a children's trust, following the exercise of options to acquire shares. The company recently reported strong Q4 2025 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations, and revised its fiscal 2026 revenue guidance upward, though analyst reports indicate the stock may be overvalued.

feed.title: Insider Sell: James Reid Sells Shares of Lincoln National Corp (LNC)
feed.summary: James Reid, Executive Vice President of Workplace Solutions at Lincoln National Corp (LNC), sold 3,050 shares of the company on February 18, 2026. This transaction continues a trend of more insider selling than buying at LNC over the past year. The stock is currently trading at $41.81, modestly overvalued according to its GuruFocus Value of $34.97.

feed.title: Quanta Services forecasts 2026 profit above estimates on strong AI data center-led demand
feed.summary: Quanta Services has projected its 2026 profit to exceed Wall Street estimates, driven by strong demand from AI data center infrastructure investments. The company's CEO highlighted significant opportunities arising from the convergence of various industries and robust electricity and infrastructure needs. Quanta also reported better-than-expected adjusted profit and revenue for the quarter ending December 31.

feed.title: Aristocrat expects to book US$32mln in legal cost recovery after litigation vs Light & Wonder : Croker
feed.summary: Aristocrat Leisure Ltd expects to recover approximately US$31.7 million (AUD45 million) in legal costs from its US$127.5 million intellectual-property settlement with Light & Wonder Inc. This was announced by CEO Trevor Croker at the firm's AGM, where he also outlined growth opportunities in gaming markets, new market entries like the UAE, and strategic acquisitions to enhance technology and AI capabilities. The company is committed to proactively defending its intellectual property.

feed.title: OmniAb to Report Fourth Quarter 2025 Financial Results on March 4
feed.summary: OmniAb, Inc. (NASDAQ: OABI) will release its financial results for the quarter and year ending December 31, 2025, after the U.S. financial markets close on Wednesday, March 4, 2026. The company will also host a conference call and webcast on the same day at 4:30 p.m. Eastern time to discuss these results and provide business updates.

feed.title: Kyndryl launches Cyber Defense Operations Center to unify network and security operations
feed.summary: Kyndryl Holdings Inc. has launched its Cyber Defense Operations Center in Bengaluru, India, to integrate network and security operations for global customers. This center aims to accelerate incident response and enhance IT performance amid rising AI-driven cyber risks and IT complexity. It offers AI-enabled assessments, role-based operational dashboards, and automated end-to-end operations, all integrated with Kyndryl Bridge, to provide unified visibility and improve resilience.

feed.title: Van Oene Mark sells PACB shares worth $294,318
feed.summary: Chief Operating Officer Mark Van Oene sold 184,897 shares of Pacific Biosciences of California (NASDAQ:PACB) for a total of $294,318 to cover tax obligations. Following these transactions, he directly owns 1,742,638 shares. This sale comes after the company reported better-than-expected Q4 2025 financial results, with a smaller loss per share and higher revenue than anticipated.

feed.title: Roper Technologies announces $0.91 per share dividend for April By Investing.com
feed.summary: Roper Technologies, Inc. has declared a dividend of $0.91 per share, payable on April 22, 2026, marking 12 consecutive years of dividend increases and 35 years of dividend maintenance. The company specializes in vertical software and technology-enabled products, utilizing a disciplined approach to acquisitions. This announcement follows recent Q4 2025 earnings where EPS beat expectations, but revenue slightly missed.

feed.title: L3Harris THAAD Contract Adds Context To Valuation And Momentum Story
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies secured a nearly $400 million contract to supply components for the Missile Defense Agency's THAAD system, reinforcing its role in missile defense. This contract adds context to the company's valuation, which is currently near estimated fair value, and highlights recent positive momentum with stock gains of 17.8% year to date. Investors should consider overall defense spending trends and the company's debt levels when assessing future performance.

feed.title: Constellation Completes Calpine Transaction, Powering America's Clean Energy Future
feed.summary: Constellation announced it has completed its acquisition of Calpine Corporation from Energy Capital Partners, creating the nation's largest producer of electricity. This combination unites Constellation's nuclear fleet with Calpine's natural gas and geothermal generation to deliver reliable, clean power and strengthen the U.S.'s energy leadership. The merged company will serve 2.5 million retail and business customers nationwide and aims to drive innovation in clean technologies.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Phillip Creek Sells Shares of M/I Homes Inc (MHO)
feed.summary: Phillip Creek, the former Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer of a branch of M/I Homes Inc (MHO), sold 1,805 shares of the company on February 18, 2026. This transaction leaves him with 33,123 shares. M/I Homes Inc is currently trading at $145.5 per share, with a market capitalization of $3.702 billion and a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.1, indicating it is fairly valued.
